_comment: 40 short single-turn prompts for the R0-vs-native paired equivalence sample. Chosen to be terse and deterministic-friendly at temperature=0 so any R0-vs-native delta is attributable to the WaM binding, not sampling noise. Each is one user turn; the agent system prompt forces a short answer.
tasks: What is the capital of France? One word.
What is 17 times 23?
Name the largest planet in our solar system. One word.
What is the chemical symbol for gold?
How many sides does a hexagon have?
What year did World War II end?
What is the square root of 144?
Name the author of Romeo and Juliet.
What is the freezing point of water in Celsius?
What is the capital of Japan? One word.
How many continents are there?
What is 2 to the power of 10?
Name the smallest prime number.
What gas do plants absorb from the atmosphere? One word.
What is the speed of light in vacuum, in meters per second (approximate, scientific notation)?
What is the capital of Australia? One word.
How many degrees are in a right angle?
Name the ocean between Africa and Australia.
What is the chemical formula for water?
What is 100 divided by 4?
Name the first president of the United States.
What is the largest mammal on Earth? Two words.
How many minutes are in a full day?
What is the boiling point of water at sea level in Fahrenheit?
What is the capital of Canada? One word.
Name the planet known as the Red Planet.
What is 9 factorial?
How many bones are in the adult human body?
What is the chemical symbol for sodium?
Name the longest river in the world.
What is 15 percent of 200?
What is the hardest natural substance on Earth? One word.
How many strings does a standard guitar have?
What is the capital of Egypt? One word.
Name the force that keeps planets in orbit around the sun.
What is the sum of the interior angles of a triangle, in degrees?
What is the chemical symbol for iron?
How many players are on a soccer team on the field per side?
What is the largest country by land area?
What is 7 squared?
